messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge from a focused chapter/section. Use the provided standard context pack for definitions, cross-references, and shared control philosophy, but extract candidates only from the focus section text. Treat chunks only as evidence anchors, not extraction boundaries. FOCUS SCOPE: Extract ONLY knowledge whose primary section_id equals one of these sections or starts with one of these section prefixes followed by a dot: 5.1. Use the rest of the manual only as context for definitions, cross-references, and terminology. Do not return candidates from outside the focus scope. Evidence quotes must come from the focus scope text, not from unrelated sections used only as context. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Prefer high-value operational knowledge: control sequences, enable/disable logic, reset logic, staging logic, setpoints, timers, alarm/fault diagnostic rules, commissioning/TAB checks, and application rules. Do not extract front matter, legal text, table-of-contents lines, bibliography, generic definitions without operational use, or unsupported paraphrases. If the same knowledge appears multiple times, return it once with the clearest authoritative evidence_quote. evidence_quote MUST be a verbatim contiguous substring copied from the manual and it MUST support every important fact in summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values, fault_condition, or commissioning_check. For operational_sequence, quote the complete rule sentence or table row that includes the condition and commanded behavior. For fault_diagnostic_rule, quote the alarm or fault condition itself. For commissioning_step, quote the check or verification action. A section heading alone, a half sentence, an editor instruction such as retain/delete this section, or an introductory phrase is NOT sufficient evidence. Do not use ellipses. Do not combine non-contiguous bullets. If one exact quote cannot support the candidate, make the candidate narrower or skip it. If a requirement has alternatives, split them into separate candidates unless one exact quote supports the combined item. Return at most 60 candidates. Prefer fewer high-confidence candidates with complete evidence over broad coverage with weak evidence. Keep evidence_quote concise, usually one complete sentence or one complete table row, not a paragraph. Preserve section_id and page_hint from nearby headings and [[page=...]] anchors. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason only.
[redacted: ASHRAE section text omitted from artifact]

      "section_title": "General",
      "equipment_scope": "Systems using Trim & Respond setpoint reset (e.g., VAV AHU static pressure)",
      "summary": "Trim & Respond logic resets the setpoint within SPmin to SPmax. When device is OFF, setpoint = SP0. When active, every time step T, if R ≤ I, trim setpoint by SPtrim; if R > I, respond by changing setpoint by SPres*(R-I) but no more than SPres-max.",
      "trigger_condition": "Associated device proven ON, after delay Td",
      "required_behavior": "Every time step T: if requests ≤ ignored, trim setpoint by SPtrim; if requests > ignored, respond by SPres*(R-I) capped at SPres-max.",

      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If there are more than I requests, respond by changing the setpoint by SPres*(R – I), (i.e., the number of requests minus the number of ignored requests) but no more than SPres-max.",

      "equipment_scope": "All equipment with staging",
      "summary": "Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment: Lifetime Runtime (cumulative since startup, not readily resettable) and Staging Runtime (operator resettable).",

      "required_behavior": "Define Lifetime Runtime and Staging Runtime points.",
      "configurable_values": [],

      "evidence_quote": "Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment: a. Lifetime Runtime: The cumulative runtime of the equipment since equipment start-up.       "page_hint": 50,

    {

      "title": "Lead/Lag Alternation Based on Staging Runtime",
      "section_id": "5.1.15.3",
      "section_title": "General",
      "equipment_scope": "Identical parallel staged equipment (e.g., CHW pumps, cooling towers)",
      "summary": "Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is made last stage and the one with the least hours is made lead stage.",
      "trigger_condition": "More than one equipment off or more than one on",

      "page_hint": 50,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },

      "title": "Lead/Standby Alternation for Redundant Equipment",
      "section_id": "5.1.15.4",

      "summary": "Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is made last stage and the one with the least hours is made earlier stage.",

      "section_title": "General",
      "equipment_scope": "Continuously running lead/standby equipment",
      "summary": "If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is shut off.",

(c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position).
